perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned and that all subsequent amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G shall be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned without the necessity of filing additional joint acquisition statements.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned, however, amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G may or may not be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned. Filing additional joint acquisition statements will be determined on a case-by-case basis.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty about whether all parties are bound by future amendments. Initially, it stated that amendments 'shall be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned'. The modification introduces the possibility that amendments 'may or may not be filed' for all parties, contradicting the original agreement and creating ambiguity about the obligation to jointly amend the filing. This directly contradicts the initial agreement to file jointly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned, however, amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G may or may not be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned. Filing additional joint acquisition statements will be determined on a case-by-case basis. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others, except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that all amendments "shall be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned." The changed text introduces the possibility that amendments "may or may not be filed" on behalf of each of the undersigned. The contradicted text repeats this new condition. This clearly creates a contradiction because the original obligation to jointly amend the filing is now uncertain, leading to a direct conflict regarding future obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others, except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each may or may not be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, and may or may not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others, regardless of whether he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty about the responsibility of each party regarding the filing and accuracy of the information. The original text states that each party 'shall be responsible' for their own information. The modification changes this to 'may or may not be responsible,' contradicting the original clearly defined obligation. The additional clause that this may or may not be true whether a company 'knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate' increases the uncertainly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned, however, amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G may or may not be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned. Filing additional joint acquisition statements will be determined on a case-by-case basis. The undersigned acknowledge that each may or may not be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, and may or may not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others, regardless of whether he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. DATED: April 8, 2020 SCULPTOR CAPITAL LP By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR CAPITAL HOLDING CORPORATION By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MANAGEMENT, INC. By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the original obligation of each party being 'responsible' for timely filing and accuracy with 'may or may not be responsible,' introducing uncertainty. This directly contradicts the implicit expectation of clear responsibility outlined in the contradicted text, which relies on the understanding that each party has a defined role in filing and accuracy. The added clause about whether a company 'knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate' adds further confusion, making enforcement of responsibility ambiguous. The original text creates a clear obligation, while the revised text renders it optional and uncertain. This fulfills the Structural Flaws definition of in-text contradiction.
